CHIEF_COMPLAINT: Ongoing management of type 2 diabetes, hypertension, and worsening right knee pain.
HISTORY_PRESENT_ILLNESS: 54 year old male veteran, service-connected DM2 and HTN. Diagnosed with diabetes 2015, on metformin 1000mg BID and recently started on glipizide 5mg daily three months ago. A1c was 8.2 in January, glipizide added then. BP has been "all over the place" per patient — home readings 140s to 160s systolic. Currently on amlodipine 10mg and lisinopril 20mg daily. Progressive right knee pain over the past 6 months, worse with stairs and prolonged walking. Was using naproxen but stopped due to GI upset. No trauma, no locking or giving way. Interested in cortisone injection. Denies chest pain, SOB, headache, visual changes.
REVIEW_OF_SYSTEMS: Constitutional: no fever, stable weight overall but up 5 lbs since last visit. HEENT: no vision changes. CV: denies chest pain, palpitations, or edema. Resp: no SOB or cough. GI: some heartburn, no nausea/vomiting, no blood in stool. MSK: right knee pain as described, no hip pain. Neuro: mild tingling in both feet, worse at night. Psych: reports feeling "stressed" about finances. Skin: no wounds or lesions, no foot ulcers. Endo: occasional hypoglycemic episodes in the afternoon, resolved with juice.
MEDICATION_CHANGES: See plan — empagliflozin 10mg daily added, chlorthalidone 12.5mg daily added, gabapentin 300mg qHS added, topical diclofenac gel added, naproxen discontinued.
MSE_THOUGHT_CONTENT: No delusions. No paranoia. Endorses financial and functional stressors.
RISK_FACTORS: Firearms access, Recent life stressor (job instability)
PROTECTIVE_FACTORS: Social support (wife and two teenage children), Engaged in treatment, Religious beliefs (church community)
OBJECTIVE_GENERAL: Overweight male appearing stated age, no acute distress, using a cane today.
OBJECTIVE_SYSTEM_EXAM: CV: Tachycardic at 91, regular rhythm, no murmurs or gallops. Lungs: Clear bilaterally. Abdomen: Soft, non-tender, no organomegaly. Right knee: Moderate effusion, tenderness along medial joint line, crepitus with ROM, no ligamentous laxity, negative McMurray. Feet: No ulcers, dorsalis pedis pulses palpable bilaterally. Decreased monofilament sensation bilateral plantar surfaces. Neuro: DTRs 2+ and symmetric. Decreased vibration sense bilateral feet.
OBJECTIVE_LAB_IMAGING: Labs (03/01/2026): A1c 7.8 (improved from 8.2), fasting glucose 148, BMP — Na 139, K 4.4, Cr 1.1, GFR 72. Lipids: TC 210, LDL 132, HDL 38, TG 200. UA: no proteinuria. Right knee x-ray (03/05/2026): moderate medial compartment degenerative changes, no fracture.
CLINICAL_IMPRESSION: 54 year old veteran with multiple service-connected conditions. DM improving on current regimen but still above goal of <7. HTN uncontrolled despite dual therapy. Right knee OA worsening, candidate for intra-articular injection. New finding of diabetic peripheral neuropathy on exam.
PLAN:
1. DM2: Continue metformin 1000mg BID and glipizide 5mg daily. Add empagliflozin 10mg daily (SGLT2 inhibitor — cardio-renal benefit). Nutrition consult for diabetic diet. Recheck A1c in 3 months.
2. HTN: Add chlorthalidone 12.5mg daily as third agent. Home BP monitoring log requested. Target <130/80.
3. Right knee OA: Triamcinolone 40mg intra-articular injection today (done — no complications). Refer ortho if no relief in 6 weeks. Discontinue naproxen (GI complaints) — start topical diclofenac gel PRN. PT referral for quad strengthening.
4. Diabetic neuropathy: Refer podiatry for diabetic foot care. Start gabapentin 300mg qHS, titrate as tolerated. Counseling on daily foot checks.
RETURN_VISIT_INSTRUCTIONS: Return in 6 weeks to reassess BP on new regimen and knee response to injection. Return sooner for any hypo episodes, worsening knee, or new foot concerns.
ORDERS_ENTERED: Labs (A1c and BMP in 3 months, lipid panel). New meds: empagliflozin 10mg daily, chlorthalidone 12.5mg daily, gabapentin 300mg qHS, topical diclofenac gel.
